heading: Rank: Chief of Staff of the Army; Chief of Naval Operations; Chief of Staff of the Air Force; Commandant of the Marine Corps; Chief of Space Operations
label: 743 Rank: Chief of Staff of the Army; Chief of Naval Operations; Chief of Staff of the Air Force; Commandant of the Marine Corps; Chief of Space Operations
text: The Chief of Staff of the Army, the Chief of Naval Operations, the Chief of Staff of the Air Force, the Commandant of the Marine Corps, and the Chief of Space Operations rank among themselves according to dates of appointment to those offices, and rank above all other officers on the active-duty list of the Army, Navy, Air Force, Marine Corps, and Space Force, except the Chairman and the Vice Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7435:626c(b)., ; , .July 26, 1947, ch. 343, § 208(b)61 Stat. 503Sept. 19, 1951, ch. 407, § 40265 Stat. 3335:626c(b) (1st sentence) is omitted as superseded by sections 8031(a)(1) and 8034(a) of this title. 5:626c(b) (2d sentence) is omitted as covered by . 5:626c(b) (3d and 4th sentences) is omitted as executed. 5:626c(b) (5th sentence) is omitted as covered by . 5:626c(b) (proviso of last sentence) is omitted as executed, since the incumbents to whom it is applied no longer hold the offices mentioned. The exception as to the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff is included because of . The words “and the Marine Corps” are inserted, since under the Chief of Naval Operations takes precedence over all other officers of the naval service.section 8034(d) of this titlesection 8034(b) of this titlesection 142(c) of this titlesection 5081 of this title

Amendments2021—, amended section catchline generally. Prior to amendment, section catchline read as follows: “Rank: Chief of Staff of the Army; Chief of Naval Operations; Chief of Staff of the Air Force; Commandant of the Marine Corps”.Pub. L. 116–283, § 924(b)(20)(B), substituted “the Commandant of the Marine Corps, and the Chief of Space Operations” for “and the Commandant of the Marine Corps”.Pub. L. 116–283, § 924(b)(20)(A), substituted “Marine Corps, and Space Force” for “and Marine Corps”.Pub. L. 116–283, § 924(b)(1)(H)1987—, inserted “; Commandant of the Marine Corps” after “Air Force” in section catchline.Pub. L. 100–180, § 1314(b)(5)(A), made technical correction in directory language of . See 1986 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 100–180, § 1314(a)(2)Pub. L. 99–4331986—, as amended by , inserted reference to the Commandant of the Marine Corp and the Vice Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff.Pub. L. 99–433Pub. L. 100–180, § 1314(a)(2)1980— substituted “active-duty list” for “active list”.Pub. L. 96–513

Effective Date of 1987 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–180, div. A, title XIII, § 1314(e)(1)101 Stat. 1176Dec. 4, 1987“The amendments made by subsection (a) [amending this section, sections 2431 to 2434 of this title, and provisions set out as notes under sections 111 and 3033 of this title] shall apply as if included in the enactment of the Goldwater-Nichols Department of Defense Reorganization Act of 1986 ().”Public Law 99–433
